strengths: Deep TypeScript, Node.js, and React expertise directly aligned with the primary stack, demonstrated across multiple senior roles including current position at re:cinq
Active AI agent and MCP (Model Context Protocol) infrastructure work in current role directly satisfies the emerging AI agent development requirement
Strong engineering discipline with consistent TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices across 15+ years and diverse domains
concerns: No explicit NestJS experience mentioned anywhere in the resume, which is a core backend framework in the required stack
Kotlin/Java exposure is narrow and dated — limited to embedded/robotics contexts at Altom (2016–2018), not backend service development
Vitest and Playwright are not mentioned; testing experience appears rooted in TDD methodology and D-language tooling rather than the specified JavaScript testing frameworks
response.justification: The candidate presents a strong match on the core fullstack profile — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker/Kubernetes — with meaningful depth and consistent seniority across roles. The current position at re:cinq adds rare, directly relevant AI agent and MCP tooling experience that few candidates will have. The main gap is NestJS, which is the specified backend framework; given the candidate's demonstrated ability to adopt new frameworks quickly across a broad technology history, this is a learnable gap rather than a structural mismatch, but it warrants validation during technical screening. Kotlin/Java depth is thin and the JVM-backend secondary requirement is unlikely to be met without additional ramp-up.
